name: cross-margin-view
description: Explore how cross-asset margin behaves on Solana. A borrower holds two collateral positions in different mints (e.g. a sovereign bond and a stablecoin) and one cross-mint loan. The unified margin view aggregates: haircut-adjusted collateral pool by asset class, projected liability (principal + simple interest), and net free margin per cash mint. Useful for asking: how does the pool change as I tighten the EQUITY haircut? What happens to free margin when ETH bounces between $1.00 and $1.08? Where does a real Pyth feed change the verdict vs the manually-set price?
headline: One borrower, two collateral mints, one cross-mint loan: free margin recomputes in one read-only call.

steps.explanation: Initialize the RiskParams PDA (one-time per deployment). Sets the governance-mutable haircut table.
(Optional) Tighten the EQUITY-class haircut from the 30% default to 45%. Demonstrates the parameter dial.
Read the current haircut table to confirm. Read-only — no keypair needed.

Assume the borrower already deposited 500_000 units of MINT_SOVEREIGN as collateral via `ssr-cli vault deposit ...` and opened a loan via `ssr-cli lending open ...` (these flows live in adjacent scenarios). Read the unified margin view across both collateral mints.
(Optional — when Pyth is bound) Refresh the collateral feed from Pyth.
Re-read margin after the Pyth refresh — observe how the oracle drives the free-margin number.
